skill.name: docs-navigation
skill.description: Navigate hierarchical ai-docs indexes to find focused documentation before broad web searches. The skill helps agents choose indexes, match keywords, and load only relevant pages.
skill.summary: Find the right local documentation page through hierarchical ai-docs indexes.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, network, and path traversal patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown examples, inline code spans, documentation URLs, and relative documentation paths, not executable code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code spans, fenced examples, and documentation path references. They do not execute commands or define runnable scripts.
low_findings.recommendation: No security change is required. Keep examples clearly fenced as documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown text and examples. No executable file, script block, or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Patterns Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in prose, metadata examples, or documentation structure examples. No hashing, encryption, checksum verification, or cryptographic API usage is implemented by this skill.
low_findings.recommendation: No cryptography warning is needed for marketplace users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain only Markdown instructions and sample TOON records. There is no code path that uses a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Paths Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The traversal alerts come from related page examples that use relative links inside the ai-docs hierarchy. They are documentation navigation examples, not filesystem reads from untrusted input.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep relative links scoped to documented ai-docs examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequence appears in a fenced page summary example. It is not used by executable code or file APIs.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Do Not Perform Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts identify example source and homepage fields for documentation pages. The skill does not instruct an agent to send data to those URLs or make network requests.
low_findings.recommendation: No publication block is needed. Treat these URLs as documentation metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside sample TOON metadata fields. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, or exfiltration behavior.

content.value_statement: Large documentation sets can waste context and slow answers. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through local indexes to load focused pages first.
actual_capabilities: Chooses the correct ai-docs index level from available task context.
Matches task keywords against index descriptions, keywords, purposes, and common tasks.
Guides agents to load specific page summaries instead of full documentation contexts.
Explains when full-context.md is justified for migrations or deep learning.
Provides cookbook guidance for level selection, keyword matching, and page loading.
limitations: Requires an ai-docs hierarchy with index files and page summaries already present.
Does not fetch, generate, or update documentation sources by itself.
May miss relevant pages when index keywords are incomplete or outdated.
Does not verify whether referenced documentation content is current.

use_cases.title: Find API Details Quickly
use_cases.description: Locate a focused documentation page for a feature, error, or syntax question without loading full library context.

use_cases.title: Prepare Migration Research
use_cases.description: Decide when targeted pages are enough and when a full-context document is justified for a broader migration.

use_cases.title: Reduce Agent Context Waste
use_cases.description: Standardize how Claude, Codex, and Claude Code search local documentation before using broader sources.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Known Library Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup with a named library
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-navigation to find the best local documentation page for BAML error handling. Start from the correct index.

prompt_templates.title: Search by Feature Keywords
prompt_templates.scenario: Topic search when the exact page is unknown
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-navigation to search local ai-docs for streaming responses. Match keywords and load only the most relevant pages.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Related Documentation Pages
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task needing several focused pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-navigation to find pages about retry behavior, timeouts, and fallback handling. Explain why each page is relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Documentation Loading Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task with context budget tradeoffs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docs-navigation to plan the minimum documentation set for a library migration. State when full-context.md is justified.

output_examples.input: Find documentation for configuring BAML streaming.
output_examples.output: Start at the BAML library index, follow the common streaming task to the guide section, then load the streaming page summary.

output_examples.input: I need help with LLM retry failures and fallbacks.
output_examples.output: Use keywords retry, failure, fallback, error, and timeout. Load the matching error-handling page before considering broader context.

output_examples.input: Should I load full-context.md for a quick syntax question?
output_examples.output: No. Load the relevant index and page summary first. Reserve full-context.md for migrations, tutorials, or deep learning tasks.

best_practices: Start at the highest known index level instead of guessing paths.
Use primary keywords and synonyms when matching task language to index fields.
Load full-context.md only when the task truly needs comprehensive documentation.
anti_patterns: Loading every documentation file before identifying the needed topic.
Skipping indexes and guessing page paths from memory.
Using web search before checking curated local documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown guidance for navigating local documentation indexes and page summaries.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: No. Example URLs appear as documentation metadata, but the skill does not make requests.

faq.question: What documentation format does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects ai-docs folders with TOON index files and page summary files.

faq.question: When should full-context.md be loaded?
faq.answer: Use it for migrations, comprehensive tutorials, architecture reviews, or deep learning of a library.

faq.question: Can it find docs when I know only a topic?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to extract keywords and match them against indexes.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that can read local documentation files.
